taskRecovery.title: Recoverable tasks detected
taskRecovery.description: On startup we found background tasks that were interrupted before the server restarted. They will not auto-resume — review each one and decide whether to resume.

toasts.resumeAllEmpty: There are no tasks to recover.

desktopBootstrap.shellNote: This screen only appears briefly while the desktop app starts, bridging the local-service launch so you don't see a blank window.
desktopBootstrap.logsHint: If startup hangs, the local service quits early, or you want to measure startup time, check the desktop logs directly.

progressHints.launching: Preparing the desktop runtime and startup resources.
progressHints.startingServer: The desktop app needs to start the local service first before entering the main workspace.
progressHints.loadingUi: Local service is available — switching to the main workspace.
progressHints.error: Something went wrong during startup. Check the logs before retrying.

hints.portable: Portable builds need a fresh installer downloaded and replaced manually.
hints.devBuild: Dev runs don't connect to the release channel; packaged installs check the desktop version automatically.
hints.idleError: When startup is blocked, the desktop version is checked in parallel so you can install a fix first.
hints.idleDefault: Before entering the workspace we check the desktop version — any available release shows up here.
hints.checking: Checking the version — we'll prompt you to download when an update is available.
hints.updateAvailable: Desktop {{version}} is available. We recommend downloading the update first.
hints.downloading: Downloading the update — please keep the app open.
hints.downloaded: Update downloaded — restart the app to finish installing.
hints.notAvailable: Your installed version matches the release channel.
hints.errorFallback: Version check failed — try again later.

desktopLegacyImport.descriptionSuggested: The desktop app detected the local database from your previous web/dev build. Import it in one click to keep your existing novels, API keys, and knowledge data.
desktopLegacyImport.descriptionManual: The desktop app uses its own data directory by default. If you already have local data from the web/dev build, you can pick the old dev.db and import it.
desktopLegacyImport.backupNote: Before import we'll back up your current desktop database to: {{path}}
desktopLegacyImport.closeOldProcess: Close any running web/dev processes before importing so the SQLite file isn't being written to.
desktopLegacyImport.importScheduled: Preparing to import legacy data. The app will restart once.

desktopModelSetup.settingsDescription: Finish at least one active model provider and default model here before returning to the main workspace.
desktopModelSetup.settingsBody: The packaged desktop app is running normally, but it still needs a usable provider configuration.
desktopModelSetup.blockerTitle: Set Up A Model Provider First
desktopModelSetup.blockerDescription: The Windows desktop app is installed correctly, but it cannot start the writing workflow until at least one model provider and default model are configured.
desktopModelSetup.blockerHint: Open the existing settings page, fill in an API key or local endpoint, choose a default model, and save it.

novelWorkspaceRail.progressDialogDescription: See the advancement steps, recent progress, and AI usage for this book.

summary.interrupted: The background task was interrupted — open execution details to see why.
summary.noActiveTask: No background director task — feel free to keep writing manually.

errors.noActiveTask: There's no auto-director task to resume.

serverStartup.title: Connecting to the local writing service
serverStartup.description: The page is ready — it'll switch to the workspace as soon as the service is available.

mobileSiteShell.moreDescription: Pick the workspace you want to continue with.

llmSelector.noProvidersHelper: No active model providers are configured. Open System Settings to set an API key and choose a model.

aiRevisionWorkspace.instructionPlaceholder: Describe the revision — e.g. trim filler, raise conflict, keep existing facts intact.
aiRevisionWorkspace.selectionHint: With no selection, only full-text optimization is available.

approvalPoints.summaryNone: No approval points will be auto-passed

approvalStrategy.fullAutoDescription: Auto-advance across the target scope; only stops for unavailable models, service errors, protected content, or unrecoverable risks.
approvalStrategy.copilotDescription: Use advanced approval authorizations to let low-risk nodes through; everything else waits for your judgment.
approvalStrategy.summaryFullAuto: Auto-advance: the system will auto-confirm planning, chapter execution, quality repair, and required replanning inside the target scope.
approvalStrategy.summaryCopilot: Copilot confirmation boundary: {{summary}}. Other approval points wait for your confirmation.

cockpit.title: AI cockpit
cockpit.fallbackReason: No AI auto-advance task needs your attention.

workerDetail.queued: Queued — the background worker will continue once it picks up.
workerDetail.running: The background worker is processing the current task.
workerDetail.stale: After an interruption, the worker will try to resume from the latest progress.
workerDetail.noBackgroundAction: No background actions are queued or running.
workerDetail.label: Background worker

circuitBreaker.title: Auto-advance paused
circuitBreaker.fallback: The system detected that continuing auto-advance may keep failing.

runtimeProjection.title: Director progress
runtimeProjection.attentionFallback: Please handle the current stopping point.
runtimeProjection.qualityDebt: Quality debt to recover{{detail}}. The system will keep writing future chapters and recover these issues during the quality repair stage.
runtimeProjection.qualityBudget: {{chapter}} quality budget: local repair {{patch}}/1, full rewrite {{rewrite}}/1, window replan {{replan}}/1. {{nextAction}}

rootCauses.replan: The current issue stems from misaligned chapter responsibilities — nearby chapters need adjusting first.
rootCauses.obligation: The draft is written, but obligations for this chapter are still unmet.
rootCauses.repairExhausted: The draft is written, but after auto repair there are still blocking issues to resolve.
rootCauses.draftMissing: The draft was not produced successfully — the current chapter needs to run again.

runtimeProjection.autopilotRecoverable: The AI can resume from the current progress.

bindingPanel.documentDescription: Once bound, these will be the default knowledge source for this entity.

documentPicker.autoHint: Using the auto rule: prefer documents bound to the entity, otherwise fall back to all enabled documents.
documentPicker.selectedCount: Selected {{count}} documents. Leaving this empty explicitly disables knowledge retrieval.
